key_insight: RetiBrain uses retinal fundus photos to estimate MRI-derived brain-health biomarkers, linking eye images to indicators of neurodegeneration and cerebrovascular injury.
clinical_relevance: This could enable cheaper, scalable, noninvasive population screening and longitudinal monitoring of brain-health risk without immediate MRI access.
method: Cross-modal deep learning distills MRI-based latent representations into a fundus-photo model.
Predicts six WMH- and hippocampal-related biomarkers from color fundus photographs.
Uses structural, topological, and geometric retinal features to improve interpretability.
evidence_signal: In a CFP-MRI paired cohort, the model improved mean Pearson correlation from 0.240 to 0.549 over RETFound, reached 0.640 for periventricular WMH prediction, and in a longitudinal cohort of 2,082 participants achieved dementia prediction AUROC 0.824.
watch_item: Performance comes from paired-cohort retrospective evaluation, so external validation and prospective testing are needed before real-world deployment.
paper_title: Retina-derived Quantitative Biomarkers of Brain Health
